# Feasibility & SSbD De-risking Matrix {#sec-ssbd-derisking-matrix}

::: {.chapterabstract}
Draft abstract: this chapter closes Part II with the feasibility layer, extending the EU **Safe and Sustainable by Design (SSbD)** framework into a scoring matrix, illustrated with two case studies.
:::

::: {.keywords}
SSbD &middot; chemical safety &middot; carbon footprint &middot; TRL risk &middot; de-risking
:::

## 6.1 The Safe and Sustainable by Design Paradigm
- Extending EU Green Digital Transformation guidance to global value chains, beyond its original EU-internal scope.

## 6.2 Case Study 1: Technologies
- Patent mapping to identify non-toxic chemical alternatives (e.g., PFAS replacement and Isopropyl Alcohol (IPA) in semiconductor manufacturing).

## 6.3 Case Study 2: Infrastructure
- De-risking next-generation data-center cooling procurement using scientometric foresight.

## 6.4 The De-risking Matrix
- Three-axis scoring: chemical toxicity x carbon footprint x TRL risk.
- Output format designed to feed directly into the DPP schema (@sec-digital-product-passports).

::: {.callout-tip title="Bridge to Part III"}
Part III operationalizes everything above into standards, data-space governance, and trade-policy alignment.
:::
